architectureVision.statement: Enable Nutrition through an integrated enterprise architecture spanning business, application, data, and technology domains.
drivers: Reduce fragmentation across organizational and technology change.
Make dependencies and ownership explicit across architecture layers.
Improve governance of strategic change and target-state decisions.

goals.name: 2026-Q1 IT Manager outcome goal
goals.description: Improve Operational reliability score (uptime + incident impact) for IT Manager by advancing harden the platform: security, sso/rbac, integration reliability, and operational observability. through trusted platform foundations.

goals.name: 2026-Q1 Executive / Economic Buyer (CEO, COO, GM) outcome goal
goals.description: Improve Time-to-value (first verified outcome) for Executive / Economic Buyer (CEO, COO, GM) by advancing prove roi fast: deploy the minimum set of workflows that show savings, risk reduction, and faster cycle times. through value realization and executive visibility.

goals.name: 2026-Q2 IT Manager outcome goal
goals.description: Prepare to improve Operational reliability score (uptime + incident impact) for IT Manager by advancing harden the platform: security, sso/rbac, integration reliability, and operational observability. through trusted platform foundations.

goals.name: 2026-Q2 Executive / Economic Buyer (CEO, COO, GM) outcome goal
goals.description: Prepare to improve Time-to-value (first verified outcome) for Executive / Economic Buyer (CEO, COO, GM) by advancing prove roi fast: deploy the minimum set of workflows that show savings, risk reduction, and faster cycle times. through value realization and executive visibility.

principles: Business capabilities drive application and technology design.
Information is modeled as a shared enterprise asset.
Technology choices should be standardized unless a domain-specific need justifies variation.
Implementation should be governed against explicit target and transition architectures.

stakeholders.name: Nutritionist / Formulation Manager (Power User)
stakeholders.concern: Owns day-to-day formulation decisions and is a key influencer on tools, data quality, and process changes.

stakeholders.name: Operations / Production Manager
stakeholders.concern: Responsible for meeting the production plan while handling real-world constraints on the shop floor.

stakeholders.name: Quality & Compliance Manager
stakeholders.concern: Ensures products meet specs and regulatory requirements with strong traceability and audit readiness.

stakeholders.name: Mother Earth (Environmental Stakeholder)
stakeholders.concern: Represents environmental sustainability, ecological impact and long-term planetary health in decision-making.

stakeholders.name: Student (Food & Feed Sciences)
stakeholders.concern: Undergraduate or graduate student in animal nutrition, food science, or feed technology using formulation and quality tools for learning and research.

stakeholders.name: Education Institution (University / Research Center)
stakeholders.concern: Universities and applied research institutes teaching and researching nutrition, feed technology, and food production systems.

stakeholders.name: Product Team (Internal)
stakeholders.concern: Owns roadmap, positioning, and long-term product strategy; balances market needs with platform coherence.

stakeholders.name: Sales Team (Internal)
stakeholders.concern: Owns pipeline and deal execution; needs credible value stories, proof, and low-friction buying motions.
